sections.paragraph:
ἦ adv. truly; the word is perh. so to be accented 1 Cor 9:10, 15 (Just.; also Ath., R. 53, 7 al.). Hb 6:14 v.l. ἦ μήν (Hom. et al.; Jos., Ant. 15, 368; 17, 72) indeed (for similar sounding εἰ μήν). Hs 9, 17, 5 Funk writes μᾶλλον δὲ ἦ χείρονες. The right rdg. prob. is μ. δ. καὶ χ. GJs 19:3 pap οὐ μὴ πιστεύσω η (interrog. ἦ B-D-F §440, 3) παρθένος ἐγέννησεν (opp. codd. … πιστεύσω ‹ὅτι› ἡ παρθένος ἐγ. [on this pass. s. deStrycker]).—Denniston 279–88. DELG. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

perh. perh. = perhaps

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Funk Funk , s. B-D-F—List 6

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

interrog. interrog. = interrogative

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

Denniston Denniston = JD., The Greek Particles—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 433
